_comment: CANONICAL SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the train to crazy town. Every representation is generated from or reads this file: allocate.py (portfolio math) reads `orgs` + `stops` directly; diagram/train_tree.json (the draw.io graph) is generated from `stops`/`edges`/`orgs`; squiggle/base_model.squiggle (slate block) and squiggle/nodes/*.squiggle (per-node assumptions) are generated from `orgs` + `stops`. DO NOT hand-edit the generated files - edit this one and run `python3 data/generate.py`; `python3 data/test_sync.py` asserts the three stay in sync. Numbers keep their provenance: animal/invertebrate `x_givewell` figures are from published EA Forum CEAs (see each org's `source`/`source_url`); human, future and x-risk figures are illustrative placeholders. squiggle `daly_per_usd` are order-of-magnitude BOTECs (`[lo, hi]` = lognormal 90% CI). The two models are deliberately different valuation lenses over the SAME inputs, so their computed winners can differ; `top_pick` is the illustrative narrative winner shown on the diagram.

stops.label:
Parochial
community + nation
stops.desc: Least-crazy end of the line. Moral weight only on people near me; heavy pure-time discount; humans only; only near-certain, measurable effects count. Under these assumptions distant strangers, animals and the future are discounted to ~0, so the local/civic option wins. The pre-EA commonsense baseline; no single EA figure defends it.

stops.label:
All present
humans
stops.desc: Flip: drop the nationality discount (Peter Singer's famine argument is the origin). A life saved abroad counts like a life saved at home, so cheap, measurable global-health and cash interventions dominate. Alexander Berger is the prominent exemplar of deliberately getting off the train here and staying with robust, evidence-backed global health & wellbeing (GiveWell / Open Phil).

stops.label:
Farmed
animals count
stops.desc: Flip: extend the moral circle to farmed vertebrates, weighted by a sentience-adjusted welfare range. Peter Singer (Animal Liberation) is the canonical articulator; Lewis Bollard leads Open Phil's farm-animal-welfare work. Corporate welfare campaigns avert enormous suffering per dollar and overtake human global health under most welfare-range estimates.

stops.label:
Invertebrates
count
stops.desc: Flip: count invertebrates, with welfare range ~ (neuron count)^exponent. Brian Tomasik is the prominent voice on insect / invertebrate suffering. This is the worked example of the train's instability: sweeping the exponent from 0 to 2 swings shrimp welfare range across ~12 orders of magnitude and flips the ranking (edit neuron_exponent in the node's Squiggle model to watch the top pick move). It is the FORK point: the tree splits here into a soil-animal branch (A) and a longtermist branch (F).

stops.label:
Soil animals
count (branch A)
stops.desc: Branch A (biological micro-minds): once you count the ~10^19 soil animals affected by land use, GiveWell top charities' cropland effect works out to ~1.11 kQALY/$ (~1.74x the Shrimp Welfare Project Humane Slaughter Initiative). The 'normal' human charity leaps to ~1.11e5x GiveWell's own direct value - and its SIGN is uncertain (it could be net-negative for soil nematodes). This branch has no Squiggle model yet: there is no comparable published soil-animal BOTEC to copy, so it is not fabricated.

stops.label:
Future
people count
stops.desc: Branch F (longtermist): stop discounting future lives. Toby Ord (The Precipice) and Will MacAskill (What We Owe The Future) are the prominent longtermists; Ajeya Cotra coined the 'train to crazy town' metaphor and rides roughly to here via worldview diversification, taking the future seriously while flagging where it gets uncomfortable. Reducing global-catastrophic risk (e.g. ALLFED's civilisational-resilience work) starts to dominate present-focused giving. The fanatical x-risk tail is still refused here (accept_tiny_prob: false).

stops.label:
Astronomical
stakes
stops.desc: Last marked stop on branch F: accept Nick Bostrom's astronomical-waste argument and take small-probability / huge-payoff bets at face value; Eliezer Yudkowsky pushes AI-risk reasoning to its stark conclusions. Existential-risk reduction dominates everything. This is the far end Cotra's near-termist deliberately gets off before; ride further and you reach fanaticism / Pascal's-mugging territory.
